steps.title: Read the circle center
steps.content:
The equation
$$x^2+(y-3)^2=64$$
has center $(0,3)$ and radius $8$.

steps.title: Shift the center down
steps.content:
Moving the circle down $5$ units subtracts $5$ from the center's $y$ coordinate.
$$(0,3)\to(0,3-5)=(0,-2)$$

steps.title: Write the new equation
steps.content: A circle with center $(0,-2)$ and radius $8$ has equation $$x^2+(y+2)^2=64$$ The answer is D.
